original: Total solar eclipse occurs
Paul Pearman jumps 21 barrels on a skateboard in Augusta
Monitor, Civil War warship, is discovered by a deep sea robot
The Dionne identical quintuplets are born to Oliva-Édouard and Elzire Dionne near Callander, Ontario, the first quintuplets to survive infancy
Monkeys Able and Baker zoom 300 miles (500 km) into space on a Jupiter missile and return safely to Earth, the first animals retrieved from a space mission
Mathias Rust, an 18-year-old West German pilot, flies a Cessna from Helsinki to Moscow in an unauthorized flight and lands near Red Square, deeply embarrassing the Soviet military
Longest wheelie (David Robilliard with 5h12m33s (Channel Islands)
American aviator Linda Finch completes Amelia Earhart's ill-fated attempted around-the-world flight
In Milan, Italy, after 22 years of restoration work,Leonardo da Vinci's masterpiece "The Last Supper" is put back on display
Solar eclipse, as predicted by Greek philosopher Thales, occurs while Lydians are at war with the Medes and leads to a truce. It is one of the cardinal dates from which other dates are calculated.
Australian cricket spin bowler Jimmy Matthews takes 2 hat-tricks on same day in Triangular Tournament v South Africa at Old Trafford (3/16, 3/38); only player to achieve the feat
Surgeons complete the world's first whole eyeball transplant with a partial facial transplant for a US veteran lineman injured in an accident at NYU Langone Health
Alan Turingsubmits "On Computable Numbers" for publication, in which he sets out the theoretical basis for modern computers
Ekaterina Gordeeva, Soviet-Russian figure skater (with Sergei Grinkov - Pairs World Championship, 1986-87, 1989-90; Olympic gold, 1988 & 94), born in Moscow, Russia
Dahyun [Kim Da-hyun], South Korean singer, rapper and actress, member of Twice, born in Seongnam, South Korea
Discovery moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-G
Robert Wentorf Jr. American physicist and inventor (1st to synthesize diamonds as part of Project Superpressure), born in West Bend, Wisconsin (d. 1997)[1]
USSR Mars 3 launched, 1st spacecraft to soft land on Mars
2 Oakland A's steal home in 1st inning
Village of Blatten, in the Swiss Alps, is flattened by a glacier collapse nine days after its 300 residents and livestock were evacuated; one man missing
Carl Filtsch, Transylvanian child piano prodigy, and composer, born in Mühlbach (Sebeș), Romania (d. 1845)
Stanley B. Prusiner, American neurologist and biochemist who discovered prions (Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine 1997), born in Des Moines, Iowa
Patch Adams [Hunter Adams], American physician, comedian, activist, and clown, born in Washington, D.C.
First Black regiment, the 54th Massachusetts, leaves Boston to fight in the US Civil War
Golden Gate Bridge in San Francisco opens to vehicular traffic
Radio program "Crazy People" (later titled The Goon Show) premieres on the BBC, created bySpike Milligan
Premiere ofWalt Disney's animated cartoon in Technicolor "Melody"
Gerrit Grijns, Dutch researcher and co-discoverer of vitamin B1 (thiamine), born in Leerdam, Netherlands (d. 1944)

Milutin Milanković, Serbian astronomer and geophysicist (linked long-term changes in climate to astronomical factors affecting the amount of solar energy received at Earth’s surface), born in Dalj, Austria-Hungary (d. 1958)
Lynn Johnston, Canadian cartoonist (For Better or For Worse) and first female recipient of the Reuben Award for Outstanding Cartoonist of the Year (1985), born in Collingwood, Ontario[1]
Michael Oher, American NFL player (Baltimore Ravens, story depicted in "The Blind Side"), born in Memphis, Tennessee
Cameron Boyce, American actor (Mirrors, Jessie), born in North Hollywood, California (d. 2019)
Netherlands closes off the Zuiderzee - a new system of dams and dykes to reclaim land and provide flood protection. The country's largest hydraulic engineering project of the 20th century.
Unmanned Apollo 2 Saturn test launched into Earth orbit
Last trip (Paris to Bucharest) on the Orient Express (after 78 years)
Congressional Committee of Astronautics meets Project 7 astronauts
KingPhilip IIdispatches the Spanish Armada under the Duke of Medina-Sidonia from Lisbon, Portugal to invade England
Sting, Stewart Copeland, and Andy Summers perform together for the first time, backing Mike Howlett at a show in Paris, France; Summers is invited to join The Police
Ian Fleming, Englishauthor(James Bond novels), born in London, England (d. 1964)
Barry Commoner, American biologist and politician (his work on radioactive fallout led to 1963 Nuclear Test Ban Treaty), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 2012)
Zahi Hawass, Egyptian archeologist, Egyptologist and Minister of Tourism and Antiquities, born in Damietta, Egypt[1][2]
MLB: AL announces it is splitting into 2 divisions
Sierra Club formed byJohn Muirand others in San Francisco, for conservation of nature
Carl Larsson, Swedish painter (Midwinter Sacrifice - Swedish National Museum of Fine Arts), born in Stockholm, Sweden (d. 1919)
Walk-in-the-Water, the first steam vessel to sail on Lake Erie, is launched
Dmitri Shostakovichcompletes his 2nd Violin concert
Walk for Reconciliation takes place across the Sydney Harbour Bridge, part of Corroboree 2000, organized by the Council for Aboriginal Reconciliation
After starting MLB career going 0-for-12, New York GiantsWillie Maysconnects for his 1st major league home run in a 4-1 loss to the Boston Braves at the Polo Grounds, NYC
Super Rugby Final, Christchurch: Canterbury Crusaders claim their 5th title with a 35-25 win over the NSW Waratahs;Dan Carterlands 3 conversions & 2 penalties for the winners
Barry Bondshits his 715th career home run, passingBabe Ruthon the MLB all-time list
Louis Agassiz, Swiss-American naturalist and geologist, born in Haut-Vully, Switzerland (d. 1873)
Clough Williams-Ellis, British architect known chiefly as the creator of the Italianate village of Portmeirion in North Wales, born in Gayton, Northamptonshire, England (d. 1978)
Patricia Quinn, Lady Stephens, Northern-Irish actress, voice artist and singer (The Rocky Horror Picture Show), born in Belfast, Northern Ireland
Seong-Jin Cho, South Korean concert pianist (International Chopin Piano Competition, 2015), born in Seoul, South Korea
First indoor swimming pool opens in Goodman's Fields, London
Fire in Quebec, Canada, 1,500 houses destroyed
Azerbaijan gains independence and declares itself a Democratic Republic
Hammond scores his 1,000th cricket run of the season after 22 days
First all-color talking picture "On With the Show" is exhibited in NYC
Cyclone hits Chittagong, Bangladesh; about 1 million houses destroyed
